goal: Render a seamless 8-second 360-degree turntable of the robot at 1080p with consistent lighting.
thoughts: Keyframe the camera orbit with explicit start==end angles so the loop seams. Drop the last frame on capture (N frames, 0 to 360*(N-1)/N) to avoid a duplicate frame. Robot stands still -- no sim_control play. RTX Interactive for quality, not path-traced (8 seconds x 24-30fps would be overnight).
code:
cam = "/World/Cameras/Turntable"
create_prim(path=cam, type_name="Camera")
configure_camera(prim_path=cam, focal_length_mm=50.0, horizontal_aperture_mm=36.0)
set_timeline_range(start=0, end=240, fps=30)
N = 240  # 8s * 30fps
for i in range(N):
    angle_deg = 360.0 * i / N  # drops the duplicate at frame N
    set_keyframe(
        prim_path=cam,
        attribute_name="xformOp:rotateZ",
        time=i,
        value=angle_deg
    )
set_camera_look_at(camera_path=cam, target_path="/World/Hero", up=(0, 0, 1))
record_demo_video(
    output_path="<OUT_MP4>",
    duration_seconds=8,
    camera_path=cam,
    fps=30
)
failure_modes: Including frame N (duplicating frame 0) so the loop pops on wrap
Running sim_control play so the robot drifts during the turntable
Path-traced render pushing turntable bake to overnight when RTX Interactive suffices
